evals.prompt: Finish branch feature/000020-030-docs-runtime-fit-wording-polish with --mode local-merge into feature/improve-skill-20260618-001 for task 000020-030-docs-runtime-fit-wording-polish.
evals.expected_output: Validates the feature branch, base branch, and task directory first. In local-merge mode it does not create a PR or wait for CI. It checks out the base branch, pulls it, runs `git merge --no-ff feature/000020-030-docs-runtime-fit-wording-polish -m "Merge branch 'feature/000020-030-docs-runtime-fit-wording-polish'"`, pushes the base branch, deletes the local feature branch, verifies the merge commit subject, marks the task complete, verifies the move or empty marker commit, and returns DONE.

evals.prompt: Finish the branch in normal-pr mode after CI is green but the PR is behind the base branch.
evals.expected_output: Runs the merge-readiness gate instead of assuming green CI is sufficient. If the branch is merely behind, merges the base into the feature branch, pushes, re-verifies CI and bot review, then checks merge readiness again before `gh pr merge --delete-branch`. It does not rebase or force-push.

evals.prompt: The task is merged locally but the task directory is still under tasks/. Finish branch can skip Mark Task Complete, right?
evals.expected_output: Refuses to declare DONE. Mark Task Complete is mandatory for normal-pr and local-merge modes. It moves the task directory to tasks/completed or creates the empty marker commit when tasks/ is gitignored, verifies the completed directory and marker commit subject, and only then reports DONE.
